examples.title: Basic tag input
examples.description: Commit free-form tags with Enter or a comma delimiter.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { FormField, TagInput } from 'cinder';
</script>

<FormField
  id="basic-tag-input"
  label="Topics"
  description="Press Enter or comma to turn the current text into a tag."
>
  <TagInput placeholder="Add a topic…" />
</FormField>

examples.title: Disabled tag input
examples.description: Mirror a locked field that can be read but not changed.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { FormField, TagInput } from 'cinder';
</script>

<FormField
  id="disabled-tag-input"
  label="Protected labels"
  description="This field is disabled while the record is syncing."
  disabled={true}
>
  <TagInput defaultValue={['Synced', 'Locked']} disabled={true} placeholder="Unavailable" />
</FormField>

examples.title: Maximum tag count
examples.description: Cap the number of committed tags and surface an inline message when the limit is reached.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { FormField, TagInput } from 'cinder';
</script>

<FormField
  id="max-tag-input"
  label="Three highlights"
  description="Add up to three short highlights for this release note."
>
  <TagInput max={3} placeholder="Add a highlight…" />
</FormField>

examples.title: Pre-populated tags
examples.description: Start uncontrolled with existing tags and keep editing in place.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { FormField, TagInput } from 'cinder';
</script>

<FormField
  id="prepopulated-tag-input"
  label="Technologies"
  description="Review the initial stack and remove anything that does not apply."
>
  <TagInput defaultValue={['Svelte', 'Bun', 'TypeScript']} placeholder="Add a technology…" />
</FormField>

examples.title: Validated email tags
examples.description: Keep the value controlled and reject entries that are not email addresses.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { FormField, TagInput } from 'cinder';

  let tags = $state<string[]>([]);

  function validateEmailTag(tag: string): boolean | string {
    return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(tag) ? true : 'Enter a valid email address.';
  }
</script>

<FormField
  id="validated-tag-input"
  label="Guests"
  description="Enter one email address per guest."
>
  <TagInput
    value={tags}
    placeholder="guest@example.com"
    validate={validateEmailTag}
    onchange={(nextTags) => {
      tags = nextTags;
    }}
  />
</FormField>
